justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Pre-commit offset pattern identified as fatal flaw causing silent data loss on crash — called it 'P0 correctness bug' and 'ticking time bomb'. (2) Explicitly explains that retry logic and DLQ do not protect against pre-commit crash — 'both correctly designed — are bypassed entirely'. (3) Verdict is NOT READY. (4) Specific remediation: 'Move offset commit to after successful processing'. (5) Correctly notes observability signals are otherwise adequate — 'strong architectural bones' with 'solid foundation' for logging/metrics/health.

observations.text: Crystal-clear timeline diagram showing exactly how events are lost between offset commit and processing
Correctly identifies that DLQ is 'functionally dead' due to the commit-before-process pattern — not confused as a compensating control
Goes beyond minimum by identifying the secondary issue: once fixed, idempotency is needed for at-least-once semantics
Explicitly states contract violation: 'at-least-once delivery guarantee is broken, effectively creating at-most-once semantics'
Prioritized recommendations clearly separate blockers from nice-to-haves, avoiding false equivalence
Identifies additional failure modes (consumer group rebalance, DLQ unavailability) that compound the core defect
